title: Observability: replace eprintln with tracing everywhere
description:
**Problem**
Mixed use of `tracing` and `eprintln!`. For a "daemon for swarms", logs matter.

**Design**
- Use `tracing` everywhere (esp sync start/stop, retries, backoff, durations, remote id)
- Add structured fields for key events
- Consider counters (sync failures, queue depth, refresh latency) - even structured logs help before full prometheus

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No `eprintln!` in non-test code
- [ ] Sync events have structured tracing spans
- [ ] Key metrics visible in logs (durations, retry counts)

**Files:** grep for eprintln, likely src/daemon/*.rs, src/git/*.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Detect and surface remote force-push/divergence
description:
**Problem**
If the remote history is rewritten (force-push), sync currently treats it like a normal remote update and proceeds. There's no explicit detection or warning to the user.

**Design**
Detect non-ancestor relationships between local/remote refs and surface a structured warning/error (e.g., status payload includes `diverged=true`). Consider requiring manual `bd sync --force` for history rewrites.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Divergence detection is explicit
- [ ] User-visible warning surfaced (status/IPC)
- [ ] Tests cover force-push/divergence scenario

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/api/mod.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Installer hardening: checksum verification + rate-limit robustness
description:
## What's Wrong
Install script fetches `releases/latest` unauthenticated and downloads a tarball without any integrity verification (checksum/signature). It may also fail due to GitHub API rate limits.

## Where
- scripts/install.sh: uses https://api.github.com/.../releases/latest and grep/sed parsing
- scripts/install.sh: downloads release asset and untars with no checksum

## Why It Matters
- Security: users execute a network script piping to bash; we should verify what we download.
- Reliability: rate limiting or JSON format changes can break installs.

## Suggested Fix
- Publish checksums (e.g., SHA256SUMS) as release assets and verify before install.
- Prefer GitHub release page redirect for latest tag, or allow `BD_VERSION` override.
- Use `jq` when available; fallback to robust parsing.

## Acceptance
- Installer verifies integrity of downloaded binary.
- Installer supports explicit version pinning.

title: Release scripts: make version bump portable across macOS/Linux
description:
## What's Wrong
`just release-patch` / `just release-minor` use `sed -i` in a way that's GNU-sed specific; BSD sed (macOS outside nix) requires an extension argument.

## Where
- justfile:67-68, 87-88

## Why It Matters
Contributors running release scripts on macOS without nix will hit a broken release flow.

## Suggested Fix
- Use a portable approach: `perl -pi -e`, or detect BSD vs GNU sed.
- Consider moving release logic to `scripts/release.sh` with explicit checks.

## Acceptance
- Release scripts work on macOS + Linux without requiring GNU sed.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
IPC: unit ResponsePayload variants serialize as null (ambiguous)
description:
**Problem**
`ResponsePayload` is `#[serde(untagged)]` and includes multiple unit variants (`Synced`, `Initialized`, `Pong`, `ShuttingDown`). These serialize to JSON `null`, so clients can deserialize ambiguously.

Example symptom: `bd init` prints `synced` instead of `initialized`.

**Design**
Switch `ResponsePayload` to an explicit tagged representation (e.g. `#[serde(tag="type", content="data", rename_all="snake_case")]`), or ensure unit variants serialize as distinct strings/objects. Keep backward compatibility via versioning or dual-decode.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd init` prints `initialized` on success
- [ ] Unit ok payloads are not serialized as `null`
- [ ] JSON decoding is deterministic
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/cli/render.rs`
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
complete_sync must not drop local mutations on join failure
description:
**Problem**
In `Daemon::complete_sync`, if mutations happened during sync and `CanonicalState::join` fails, the code discards local state and keeps `synced_state`. That can drop mutations made during the sync window.

**Design**
When join fails, detect/resolution collisions (reuse `git::collision` logic) and retry the merge. If merge still fails, keep local state, mark dirty, and surface/log the failure so operators can intervene.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Local mutations are never dropped on join failure
- [ ] Collision resolution is applied for dirty-after-sync merges
- [ ] Tests cover the join-failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/git/collision.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add kill -9 crash recovery integration test for WAL
description:
Add integration test that:
1. Starts daemon
2. Creates beads
3. SIGKILL daemon (before sync completes)
4. Restarts daemon
5. Verifies beads recovered from WAL

Requires subprocess spawning and signal handling in test harness. See tests/critical_path.rs for existing integration test patterns.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Expose sync retry backoff (next retry time) in daemon/CLI status

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Type-safe daemon: eliminate invariant-checking unwraps
description:
Eliminate unwraps in daemon code by encoding proven invariants in the type system.

**Goal:** No `unwrap()` in non-test daemon paths where failures can be caused by repo state or git IO.

**Approach:** "Parse, dont validate" — move checks to where data enters the system and let types remember what has been validated.

**Subtasks:**
- Serde boundary validation for identity types
- Encapsulate DepKey (no self-loops)
- LoadedRemote proof type
- require_live helper
- with_mutation transaction helper
- Collapse live/tombstones into BeadEntry
- Remove duplicate key from DepEdge

See individual beads for design details.
closed_reason: Core goal achieved: eliminated all .unwrap() calls on bead lookups in daemon paths. Completed: serde boundary validation, DepKey encapsulation, LoadedRemote proof type, require_live helpers + refactoring, with_mutation helper. Remaining low-priority cleanup (bd-mgf, bd-di1) filed as separate beads.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Update stateright models for REALTIME_PLAN v0.5
description:
**Problem**
Stateright models in `beads_stateright_models` still reflect v0.4 realtime spec (self-hashing envelopes, unknown-key preservation, GC marker events, PENDING/COMMITTED idempotency). REALTIME_PLAN.md v0.5 simplified hashing, removed unknown-key preservation and GitCheckpointed durability, and deferred GC markers. Models should match v0.5.

**Design**
- Update `beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs` to v0.5: hash over EventBody bytes, sha/prev in headers, no unknown-key preservation, no NamespaceGcMarker, no GitCheckpointed.
- Update toy hashing helpers (`beads_stateright_models/src/toy_codec.rs`) and models (`beads_stateright_models/examples/hash_prev_machine.rs`, `beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs`) to use v0.5 hash/prev semantics.
- Update idempotency model (`beads_stateright_models/examples/idempotency_receipt_machine.rs`) to use stable idempotency mapping (no PENDING/COMMITTED receipts).
- Remove or mark GC floor model as deferred in v0.5.
- Refresh doc comments/README/todo in the models crate to reference v0.5 sections.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All models compile under v0.5 assumptions (no v0.4-only features).
- [ ] Hash/prev models verify sha over EventBody bytes and use header prev_sha semantics.
- [ ] Idempotency model reflects stable request mapping (no pending/committed states).
- [ ] GC floor example is removed or explicitly marked deferred for v0.5.
- [ ] `beads_stateright_models` docs mention v0.5 plan alignment.
- [ ] Tests (model examples) still run.

**Files:**
- beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs
- beads_stateright_models/src/toy_codec.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/hash_prev_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/idempotency_receipt_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/gc_floor_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/README.md
- beads_stateright_models/todo.md
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Note ID collisions should be guarded
description:
**Problem**
Note IDs are random 8-char base58; collisions are unlikely but possible. A collision overwrites an existing note in the CRDT map.

**Design**
Include actor + timestamp in note IDs or retry generation until unique in-bead. Add a test that forces collision handling.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Note ID generation is collision-safe
- [ ] Tests cover collision path

**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add bd dep cycles detection command
description:
beads-go has 'bd dep cycles' to detect circular dependencies in the dependency graph.

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/dep.rs

title: Remote URL canonicalization + type tightening
description:
**Problem**
Remote URL normalization is too loose (ssh/https variants, ports, users). This can split canonical state across clones and cause data divergence.

**Design**
Add a stricter RemoteUrl parser that:
- strips scheme/user, lowercases host
- normalizes ssh scp-style and ssh:// URLs
- preserves port when present (host:port)
- trims .git and trailing slashes
Add focused tests for common variants (ssh/https/ssh://, with/without user, with port).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Same remote in different URL forms maps to identical RemoteUrl
- [ ] Tests cover common cases + edge cases
- [ ] No regressions for file:// or local paths

**Files:** src/daemon/remote.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Sync fetch must not clobber local ref (ancestry check + backup)
description:
**Problem**
`SyncProcess::fetch` force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to match remote and uses `unwrap_or(Oid::zero())` for ref lookup. If local is ahead/diverged, this overwrites local commits; if ref lookup errors, we treat it as missing. Both can lose durable local history.

**Design**
- Use ref lookup that distinguishes NotFound vs error.
- Only fast-forward local ref when it is an ancestor of remote.
- If local is ahead/diverged, create a backup ref (e.g., `refs/beads/backup/<oid>`) before any update and avoid forcing local to remote.
- Keep commit parent as remote head for linear history.
- Add tests covering local-ahead and diverged scenarios.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No forced local ref update when local ahead/diverged
- [ ] Backup refs created for diverged local history
- [ ] Ref lookup errors are surfaced
- [ ] Tests cover ancestry check + backup behavior
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/git/error.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Migration: make imported comment NoteIds globally unique
description:
## What's Wrong
Go comment notes are imported with `NoteId::new(c.id.to_string())`. If comment IDs are not globally unique across issues in beads-go exports, notes can collide and overwrite/merge unexpectedly.

## Where
- src/migrate/go_export.rs:288 (NoteId::new(c.id.to_string()))

## Why It Matters
Data loss / silent merging during migration: two different comments could end up with the same note ID.

## Suggested Fix
- Make note IDs include the issue ID (and/or created_at) e.g. `go-comment-<issue-id>-<comment-id>`.
- Add a migration test fixture with repeated comment IDs across different issues to verify uniqueness.

## Acceptance
- Import never drops/merges distinct comments due to note ID collisions.
closed_reason: Made imported comment NoteIds globally unique by including issue ID in the format: go-comment-{issue_id}-{comment_id}. Added tests to verify uniqueness across issues.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Realtime v0.5 implementation
description: Implement REALTIME_SPEC_DRAFT v0.5 per REALTIME_PLAN.md. Track phases 1-7 (REALTIME_PLAN.md §19) and their dependencies.

title: Phase 1: Core identity types + StoreMeta
description:
**Problem**
Phase 1: add realtime identity atoms and StoreMeta. Current `src/core/identity.rs` only has ActorId/BeadId/NoteId and the only meta is `src/core/meta.rs` (git format). Later phases (WAL, replication, checkpoints) need StoreId/ReplicaId/TxnId/ClientRequestId/StoreEpoch/SegmentId and StoreMeta persisted to disk.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1 (StoreId identity), §2.1 (Identity + StoreMeta), §15.1 (store meta file)
- Add `uuid` dependency (REALTIME_PLAN.md §1.1)
**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/core/store_meta.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs, Cargo.toml
design:
**Design**
- Add newtypes: StoreId, ReplicaId, TxnId, ClientRequestId, StoreEpoch, SegmentId using `uuid::Uuid` (serde enabled).
- Implement Display/Debug/Serialize/Deserialize with transparent wrapper; parsing can accept UUID strings.
- Create `StoreMeta` struct with fields: store_id, store_epoch, replica_id, store_format_version, wal_format_version, checkpoint_format_version, replication_protocol_version, index_schema_version, created_at_ms.
- Provide constructors and serde round trip tests.
- Export new types from `src/core/mod.rs` and `src/lib.rs`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New types compile and serialize and deserialize correctly (unit tests).
- [ ] `StoreMeta` struct matches plan fields and is exported.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

title: Phase 3: SQLite WAL index + WalIndex traits
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: add WAL SQLite index with schema and reader and writer traits. There is no SQLite index today; WAL is a snapshot file. The realtime plan requires a rebuildable index for event lookup, idempotency, and watermarks.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11 (schema decisions) and §6.2 (table layout)
- SQLite PRAGMA choices in §6.4
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (rusqlite)
design:
**Design**
- Implement schema for events, watermarks, client_requests, origin_seq, segments, meta (per plan).
- Add WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader traits with methods from REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.1.
- Use UUIDs as 16 byte BLOBs. Store event_ids as CBOR encoded Vec<EventId> in client_requests.
- Configure SQLite with PRAGMA: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL (configurable), foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout.
- Add schema version in meta table and enforce upgrade rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Database initializes with expected tables and indices.
- [ ] Writer and reader trait methods are implemented and tested.
- [ ] Schema version is stored and validated on open.

title: Phase 3: WAL replay + index rebuild
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement WAL replay and SQLite index rebuild from WAL segments, including tail truncation rules. Without this, crash recovery, idempotency, and origin_seq allocation are unsafe.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.3 (index rebuild and catch up), §6.5 (crash consistency), §7.3 step 8 (replay)
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/replay.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement scan of wal/<namespace>/segment-*.wal in creation order.
- Validate segment headers (store_id, store_epoch, namespace, wal_format_version) before replay.
- For each segment, read frames from last_indexed_offset (if present) to EOF and index records.
- On tail corruption, truncate the segment to the last valid frame boundary and update segments table.
- Provide full rebuild path when index is missing or schema mismatch.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rebuild populates events, watermarks, origin_seq tables from WAL.
- [ ] Tail truncation is handled safely and covered by tests.
- [ ] Startup can catch up from last_indexed_offset without full scan.

title: Phase 4: MutationEngine planning + request hash
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: implement a MutationEngine that turns IPC ops into a single EventBody with canonical request hash and stamps. Current mutations in `src/daemon/executor.rs` mutate state directly; there is no event planning or idempotency digest.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §8.1 and §8.2 (mutation planning and request_sha256)
- request_sha256 storage in WAL headers (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11)
**Files:** src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs (new), src/daemon/ops.rs, src/daemon/clock.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a MutationEngine that validates inputs, enforces limits (MAX_OPS_PER_TXN, MAX_NOTE_BYTES), and builds one EventBody (TxnV1).
- Compute canonical request_sha256 from a normalized request that excludes retry only fields (wait_timeout_ms, durability wait targets).
- Mint txn_id and stamps using HLC; ensure event_time_ms equals stamp wall time and populate hlc_max.
- Return EventDraft { event_body, request_sha256, client_request_id } for WAL append.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MutationEngine produces EventBody and request_sha256 deterministically (tests).
- [ ] Limit enforcement rejects oversize requests before WAL append.
- [ ] No direct CanonicalState mutations happen in MutationEngine.

title: Phase 4: Daemon events first mutation pipeline
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: integrate the events first mutation pipeline in the daemon. Current `src/daemon/executor.rs` mutates CanonicalState directly and writes snapshot WAL. We need WAL append plus deterministic apply_event and watermark updates.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §8.3 (apply pipeline) and §6.5 (crash consistency ordering)
- StoreRuntime integration in §7.1
**Files:** src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, src/daemon/query.rs
design:
**Design**
- Replace direct mutation calls with: MutationEngine -> EventWal append -> core::apply_event -> update indexes and watermarks -> schedule replication and checkpoint.
- Enforce idempotency via WAL index lookup before creating a new event when client_request_id is present.
- Persist HLC state and idempotency mapping in the same WAL append critical section.
- Ensure responses are returned only after apply and watermark updates complete.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All mutation handlers use the events first pipeline.
- [ ] Idempotent retry returns prior txn_id and event_ids without writing a new event.
- [ ] Watermarks advance only after apply_event completes.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes for mutation tests.

title: Phase 4: IPC requests + receipts (namespace, durability, client_request_id)
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: update IPC request and response shapes for realtime fields and receipts. Current `src/daemon/ipc.rs` has no namespace, durability class, or client_request_id fields, and ErrorPayload does not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC protocol evolution), §16.1 (request fields and error payload), §16.3 (CLI flags)
**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, REALTIME_ERRORS.md
design:
**Design**
- Add optional `namespace`, `durability`, and `client_request_id` fields to mutation requests with defaults (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync).
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Plumb CLI flags `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id` to IPC requests.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IPC request structs accept the new fields without breaking existing clients (optional fields).
- [ ] Error payload matches REALTIME_ERRORS.md and includes retryable and receipt when appropriate.
- [ ] CLI passes namespace and durability flags through.

title: Phase 5: Replication protocol types + framing
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: implement replication protocol types and framing. There is no realtime replication protocol in the codebase yet.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1-9.6 (frames, HELLO/WELCOME/EVENTS/ACK/WANT/ERROR)
- Error payload rules in REALTIME_ERRORS.md
**Files:** src/daemon/repl/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/mod.rs (new), REALTIME_ERRORS.md
design:
**Design**
- Implement CBOR message envelope `{ v, type, body }` with `minicbor`.
- Define structs for HELLO, WELCOME, EVENTS (EventFrameV1), ACK, WANT, ERROR with field names matching the spec.
- EventFrameV1 carries EventId, sha256, prev_sha256, and raw EventBody bytes.
- Enforce size bounds (MAX_FRAME_BYTES, MAX_EVENT_BATCH_BYTES) during decode.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Encode and decode round trip tests for each message type.
- [ ] Error frames use the same ErrorPayload shape as IPC.
- [ ] Bounds checks reject oversized frames.

title: Phase 5: EventBroadcaster + hot cache
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: add a bounded EventBroadcaster and hot cache for recently appended events. There is no pub or sub mechanism to stream events or serve fast replication reads.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §7.5 (EventBroadcaster and hot cache requirements)
**Files:** src/daemon/broadcast.rs (new) or src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement a broadcaster that publishes EventId, sha, prev_sha, namespace, and canonical EventBody bytes.
- Enforce bounds by events and bytes; drop slow subscribers with ERROR(code="subscriber_lagged").
- Maintain a FIFO hot cache with deterministic eviction.
- Integrate publish after WAL append in StoreRuntime.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Subscriptions receive events in order and drop on lag.
- [ ] Hot cache evicts by size and event count deterministically.
- [ ] `cargo test` covers backpressure behavior.

title: Phase 5: Replication sessions + manager + server
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: implement replication runtime (sessions, manager, server). There is no peer handshake, WANT/ACK flow, or ingest path today.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.3-9.8 (handshake, EVENTS, WANT, ACK, gaps, backpressure)
- Threaded architecture in §0.13
**Files:** src/daemon/repl/session.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/manager.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/server.rs (new), src/daemon/core.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement HELLO/WELCOME negotiation and capability checks.
- Implement session state that ingests EVENTS in order, buffers gaps, issues WANT, and sends ACK with watermarks and heads.
- Enforce backpressure (bounded queue by bytes/events) and rate limits; close on overload with ERROR(code="overloaded").
- Sessions communicate with daemon coordinator via bounded channels for append and apply.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sessions enforce contiguous ingest and gap buffering rules.
- [ ] WANT and ACK flows are exercised in tests.
- [ ] Backpressure limits are enforced (no unbounded buffering).

title: Phase 5: DurabilityCoordinator + peer ACK tracking
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: implement DurabilityCoordinator for LocalFsync and ReplicatedFsync(k). There is no coordinator to track peer ACKs or advance durable watermarks beyond local fsync.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §10 (durability coordination), §0.12 (applied vs durable watermarks), §2.1.2 (replica roster)
**Files:** src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs (new), src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/repl/*
design:
**Design**
- Track pending txns by txn_id and required durability class.
- For ReplicatedFsync(k), select eligible replicas (from roster if present) and wait for durable ACKs.
- On success, return updated receipt with durability_proof and advance durable watermarks.
- On insufficient eligible replicas, return ERROR(code="durability_unavailable").
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReplicatedFsync(k) completes when k eligible replicas ACK.
- [ ] Insufficient eligible replicas triggers immediate error.
- [ ] Durable watermark advances only after local fsync and required ACKs.

title: Phase 6: Checkpoint canonical JSON + manifest/meta structs
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement canonical JSON helper and checkpoint manifest and meta structs. There is no checkpoint lane module yet, so manifest and meta definitions do not exist.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.3-13.5 (canonical JSON, manifest.json, meta.json)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/json_canon.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/manifest.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `to_canon_json_bytes<T: Serialize>(v: &T) -> Vec<u8>` with sorted key ordering and no extra whitespace.
- Define Manifest and Meta structs with fields from plan; implement content_hash and manifest_hash calculation.
- Add tests verifying canonical JSON stability and hash determinism.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical JSON helper emits stable bytes across runs.
- [ ] manifest_hash and content_hash computed per plan.
- [ ] Tests cover a minimal checkpoint meta example.

title: Phase 1: Namespaces + StoreState
description:
**Problem**
Phase 1: add namespace and StoreState types. `CanonicalState` is single namespace today; the plan requires `StoreState = BTreeMap<NamespaceId, CanonicalState>` plus namespace policy types. No NamespaceId or policy structs exist yet.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.3 (StoreState choice), §0.4 (namespace policy), §2.2 (Namespaces)
**Files:** src/core/namespace.rs (new), src/core/store_state.rs (new) or src/core/state.rs, src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add `NamespaceId` newtype with validation `[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,31}` and helpers (as_str, display).
- Add `NamespacePolicy` and `CheckpointGroup` config structs and default policies (core/sys/wf/tmp) from REALTIME_PLAN.md §2.2.
- Implement `StoreState` as `BTreeMap<NamespaceId, CanonicalState>` with helpers: get, get_mut, ensure_namespace, and a constant default namespace "core".
- Export new types from `src/core/mod.rs` and `src/lib.rs`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] NamespaceId validation tests cover valid and invalid cases.
- [ ] StoreState compiles and can create and access the default core namespace.
- [ ] No daemon behavior changes yet; only core types and exports.

title: Phase 6: Checkpoint export + cache
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement checkpoint export to sharded JSONL plus local cache. There is no exporter for the new checkpoint layout.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.1, §13.3, §13.8 (layout, sharding, export, cache)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/export.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/layout.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement sharded JSONL layout under namespaces/<ns>/{state,tombstones,deps}/00..ff.jsonl.
- Serialize lines in canonical JSON with deterministic sorting by key.
- Write manifest.json and meta.json with hashes and included watermarks.
- Add local checkpoint cache under store_dir/checkpoint_cache/<group>/ with atomic publish (tmp + rename + fsync).
- Add tests verifying shard ordering and manifest hashes for a small StoreState.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Export writes shard files, manifest.json, and meta.json with correct hashes.
- [ ] Cache publish is atomic and uses temp dirs.
- [ ] Tests cover deterministic export for a tiny StoreState.

title: Phase 6: Checkpoint import + verification
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement checkpoint import and verification (manifest_hash and content_hash). There is no importer for the new checkpoint layout.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.9 and §13.9.1 (import, verification, JSONL bounds)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/import.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/layout.rs, src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs
design:
**Design**
- Read checkpoint tree or cache, verify manifest hashes and file hashes.
- Validate content_hash from meta preimage.
- Parse JSONL shards line by line with size bounds and merge into StoreState deterministically.
- Return included watermarks and optional included_heads for seeding head sha.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Import rejects mismatched hashes.
- [ ] JSONL parsing enforces line and shard size limits.
- [ ] Merge output is deterministic and tested with a fixture.

title: Phase 6: Checkpoint scheduler + git ref wiring
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: integrate checkpoint scheduling and git ref wiring. Export and import modules alone are not used until daemon schedules checkpoints and writes the new refs.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.2 (refs), §14 (scheduler), §7.3 (startup flow)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/*, src/daemon/scheduler.rs or new checkpoint runtime module, src/daemon/git_worker.rs, src/git/sync.rs (legacy)
design:
**Design**
- Add checkpoint runtime that tracks dirty namespaces and schedules exports with debounce, max interval, and max events.
- Write checkpoint commits to `refs/beads/<store_id>/<group>` and keep `refs/beads/meta` for discovery.
- Ensure only one push in flight per group and coalesce when new events arrive.
- Keep legacy `src/git/sync.rs` as read only migration path.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Checkpoint export is scheduled and produces commits under new refs.
- [ ] Dirty events during export schedule a follow up checkpoint.
- [ ] Legacy sync remains available for migration but does not write new realtime refs.

title: Phase 7: IPC Subscribe streaming + require_min_seen
description:
**Problem**
Phase 7: implement IPC Subscribe streaming with require_min_seen gating. IPC is currently request or response only and has no streaming path.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §11 (local subscriptions), §16.1 (require_min_seen and timeout semantics)
**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/daemon/server.rs, src/daemon/broadcast.rs, src/api/mod.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a Subscribe request variant with namespace, require_min_seen, and wait_timeout_ms.
- Stream EventBody bytes plus sha and prev_sha headers.
- Use EventBroadcaster for live updates and WAL read for historical backfill.
- If require_min_seen is not satisfied, wait up to wait_timeout_ms then return retryable error with current watermarks.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Subscribe streams events in order and closes on disconnect.
- [ ] require_min_seen gating works and returns retryable errors on timeout.
- [ ] Backpressure limits prevent unbounded buffering.

title: Phase 1: Watermarks + durability types
description:
**Problem**
Phase 1: add watermark and durability receipt types. Later phases need applied and durable watermarks plus durability classes and receipts for IPC and replication. No watermark or durability structs exist yet.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.12 (applied vs durable watermarks), §10 (Durability classes), §16.1 (receipt shape)
**Files:** src/core/watermark.rs (new), src/core/durability.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add `WatermarkMap = BTreeMap<NamespaceId, BTreeMap<ReplicaId, u64>>` and `WatermarkHeadMap = BTreeMap<NamespaceId, BTreeMap<ReplicaId, [u8; 32]>>` with helpers (get, merge_max, advance_contiguous).
- Add `DurabilityClass` enum: LocalFsync and ReplicatedFsync(k).
- Add `DurabilityReceipt` with txn_id, event_ids, min_seen (applied watermarks), and durability_proof (local fsync and optional replicated acks). Keep it serde friendly for IPC.
- Export new types from `src/core/mod.rs` and `src/lib.rs`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Watermark helper tests cover get and merge behavior.
- [ ] DurabilityClass and DurabilityReceipt serialize and deserialize.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

title: Phase 2: Core wire delta types
description:
**Problem**
Phase 2: define core wire delta types for EventBody deltas. Only legacy git wire structs exist today (`src/git/wire.rs`), which are tied to the checkpoint lane. Realtime events need core wire types with stable field names for bead/deps/notes operations.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.7 (delta schema choice) and §2.4 (DeltaV1 fields)
**Files:** src/core/wire_bead.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/git/wire.rs (reference only)
design:
**Design**
- Add core wire structs: WireBeadPatch (and optional WireBeadFull), WireDepUpsert/WireDepDelete, WireBeadDelete, WireNoteAppend, matching field names from `src/git/wire.rs` for compatibility.
- Encode stamps using existing `Stamp`/`WriteStamp` and ActorId types.
- Enforce notes rule: patch deltas should omit notes by default; if notes are present, treat as set union only.
- Provide conversions between core domain types (Bead, Tombstone, DepEdge, Note) and wire structs where lossless.
- Add serde round trip tests for the new wire types.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New core wire structs compile and are exported.
- [ ] Field names match legacy wire naming for compatibility.
- [ ] Conversion and serde tests pass.

title: Phase 2: EventBody canonical CBOR + hashing
description:
**Problem**
Phase 2: implement EventBody for realtime events with canonical CBOR encoding and hashing. There is no EventBody type or canonical encoding path today, which blocks WAL/replication hashing guarantees.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.6 (canonical CBOR and hashing), §2.4 (EventBody fields), §9.4 (EVENTS frames)
**Files:** src/core/event.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (minicbor), tests under src/core
design:
**Design**
- Define EventBody, EventKind (TxnV1), and Delta types that embed the core wire delta structs (Phase 2).
- Implement canonical CBOR encoder for EventBody using `minicbor::Encode` with definite lengths and sorted map keys. Reject floats and indefinite length for hashed types.
- Provide helper functions: encode_event_body_canonical -> Vec<u8>, hash_event_body(bytes) -> [u8; 32].
- Add decoding with bounds enforcement (depth, map entries, array entries, max bytes) as per REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19.
- Add tests for stable encoding and hash determinism across runs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] EventBody encodes to canonical bytes and hashes match expectations (unit tests).
- [ ] Decoding rejects indefinite length and out of bounds payloads.
- [ ] New module exported from `src/core/mod.rs`.

title: Phase 2: core apply_event for EventBody
description:
**Problem**
Phase 2: implement deterministic apply path for EventBody TxnV1. The daemon currently mutates CanonicalState directly; there is no pure apply function in core, which will cause divergence once replication is added.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.8 (deterministic apply semantics), §2.4 (DeltaV1), §8.3 (apply pipeline)
**Files:** src/core/apply.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/core/state.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/core/dep.rs, src/core/tombstone.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add `apply_event(state: &mut CanonicalState, event: &EventBody) -> ApplyOutcome` as a pure function with no I/O.
- Apply bead_upserts, bead_deletes, dep_upserts, dep_deletes, note_appends using CRDT join semantics (Lww, Tombstone, DepEdge).
- Enforce idempotent no op rules and detect note id collisions with mismatched content as corruption.
- Return ApplyOutcome with changed bead ids, dep keys, note ids, and watermark advance hints to support indexing.
- Add regression tests for tombstone vs update, dep delete vs restore, and idempotent reapply.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apply_event is deterministic and pure; no daemon calls.
- [ ] Tests cover idempotence and collision detection.
- [ ] Module exported from `src/core/mod.rs`.

title: Phase 3: Store dir layout + store lock
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement store directory layout and a store global lock. Current paths only expose a base data dir and WAL uses per remote snapshot filenames. Realtime needs `$BD_DATA_DIR/stores/<store_id>/` with a lock file and secure permissions.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.2 (process level locking), §15.1 (store layout), §7.3 (startup flow steps 2-4)
**Files:** src/paths.rs, src/daemon/store_lock.rs (new), src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (new) or src/daemon/core.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add path helpers in `src/paths.rs`: stores_dir, store_dir(store_id), store_meta_path, wal_dir, index_path, checkpoint_cache_dir.
- Implement a store lock file in the store dir with metadata: store_id, replica_id, pid, started_at_ms, daemon_version, last_heartbeat_ms.
- Acquire lock at store open; on failure show metadata and fail fast. No auto delete.
- Enforce file permissions (0700 for dirs, 0600 for sensitive files) and reject symlinks for critical paths.
- Expose a helper API for lock acquire and release (called by StoreRuntime open).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Store directory helpers return expected paths under BD_DATA_DIR.
- [ ] Lock file is created with required metadata and permissions; failure surfaces metadata.
- [ ] Store open fails if lock held.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

title: Phase 3: StoreRuntime + StoreId identity mapping
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: introduce StoreRuntime and move daemon identity from RemoteUrl to StoreId (hybrid mapping). Today `Daemon` is keyed by `RemoteUrl` and uses `path_to_remote` in `src/daemon/core.rs`, which blocks per store WAL and replication.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1 (StoreId as correctness identity) and §7.1-7.4 (StoreRuntime + discovery order)
- Migration fallback UUIDv5(normalized_remote_url) in §0.16
**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/remote.rs, src/daemon/repo.rs, src/paths.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `StoreRuntime` struct (initially minimal fields: meta, policies, state, watermarks, wal handle) in `src/daemon/store_runtime.rs`.
- Change `Daemon` maps to `BTreeMap<StoreId, StoreRuntime>` plus `path_to_store_id` and `remote_to_store_id` caches.
- Implement store identity discovery order: local store meta.json, refs/beads/meta store_meta.json, refs/beads/<store_id> listing, UUIDv5(normalized_remote_url) fallback for migration only.
- Keep RemoteUrl mapping for legacy git sync, but do not key correctness on it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Daemon state keyed by StoreId; remote URL only used for legacy git lane mapping.
- [ ] Store discovery order matches plan and is covered by tests or structured logs.
- [ ] Existing commands still resolve a store from repo path (compat) without behavior regression.

title: Phase 3: Event WAL framing + segment writer
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement the event WAL segment format and framing. Current `src/daemon/wal.rs` is a snapshot WAL; there is no event log, framing, or segment rotation.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.9 (segment naming), §0.10 (LocalFsync), §6.4 and §6.5 (crash consistency)
- Framing uses crc32c (REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1)
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/mod.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/segment.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/record.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Define WAL segment header with store_id, store_epoch, namespace, wal_format_version, created_at_ms, segment_id.
- Define record header with EventId fields, sha256, prev_sha256 (optional), event_time_ms, txn_id, client_request_id, request_sha256 (optional), and record_len.
- Implement frame encoding with length + crc32c and tail truncation handling.
- Implement segment rotation by size and age; create files under wal/<namespace>/segment-<created_at_ms>-<segment_id>.wal.
- Implement LocalFsync semantics: fsync segment after append; fsync directory only on new segment creation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Can append and read back a record frame with crc32c validation.
- [ ] Segment creation and rotation follow naming and fsync rules.
- [ ] MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES enforced before writing.

title: Add --type flag to bd update command
description:

Cannot change a beads type after creation. Had to close bd-d37 and recreate as epic bd-2fr.

Tombstone GC policy can allow ID reuse/resurrection
description:
**Problem**
Tombstones are GC'd after 30 days in sync merge. If an ID is resurrected after GC, collisions can reappear or deleted IDs can be reused unintentionally.

**Design**

**Acceptance**
- [ ] GC policy documented and configurable or safe by default
- [ ] Regression test for resurrection after GC

title: darin@dusk
Daemon init should not ignore ensure_repo_loaded errors; dedupe SyncWait
description:
## What's Wrong
`Request::Init` ignores errors from `ensure_repo_loaded` (TODO in code). Also `SyncWait` behavior is duplicated: `Daemon::handle_request` returns Synced immediately, but `run_state_loop` has special-case barrier logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/core.rs:585 (TODO: ignores ensure_repo_loaded errors after init)
- src/daemon/core.rs:559 (SyncWait returns immediately)
- src/daemon/server.rs:45 (SyncWait handled as a barrier before calling daemon)

## Why It Matters
Init can report success even if repo state isn't loadable, leading to confusing follow-on failures. Duplicated SyncWait logic makes correctness brittle and creates drift.

## Suggested Fix
- Make Init return an error (or include warning details) if `ensure_repo_loaded` fails.
- Consolidate SyncWait into one place (prefer state loop barrier) and remove/clarify the other path.

## Acceptance
- `bd init` fails (or returns structured warning) when it cannot load the repo state.
- `SyncWait` has a single source of truth (no duplicate paths).

- remote ahead of local (fast-forward update succeeds)

- push non-fast-forward retry loop respects max_retries
Use temp git repos with controlled commit graphs.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests cover the above scenarios
- [ ] Sync outcome divergence is surfaced
- [ ] No data loss (local commits preserved)

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Daemon load blocks on SSH auth, hangs IPC
description:
**Problem**
First access to a repo blocks the daemon in `Daemon::ensure_repo_loaded` while waiting for `GitOp::Load` to finish. `GitWorker::load` always performs a remote fetch (even in best-effort mode). If SSH auth is misconfigured (no agent / passphrase prompt / stalled handshake), libgit2 fetch can hang. Because `ensure_repo_loaded` waits synchronously, the state thread blocks and IPC requests like `bd status` hang indefinitely. We saw `bd status` time out on this machine before SSH auth was configured.

**Design**
Make load non-blocking, and treat timeouts as a guardrail rather than the primary fix:
- Split `GitWorker::load` into a fast local-only load (read local ref, compute state) and a remote refresh path.
- Change `ensure_repo_loaded` to return local state immediately and, if the repo is stale, enqueue a background `GitOp::Refresh` (or `GitOp::Fetch`) without blocking.
- Only block on remote fetch when no local ref exists; add a bounded timeout (default ~30s, configurable) and return a clean, actionable error on timeout with diagnostic info (SSH agent/auth hints, remote URL).
- Surface fetch/auth errors via `fetch_error` and Sync warnings, but keep IPC responsive.

**Design Notes**
The primary goal is responsiveness: read-only queries must not block on network or auth. Use best-effort refresh for freshness, but never let it block IPC. Timeout is a secondary safety net for unavoidable fetches. Keep existing CRDT merge semantics intact.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd status` returns promptly (e.g., <1s) when SSH auth is missing/invalid; no hang.
- [ ] If local ref exists, initial load is non-blocking and refresh happens in the background.
- [ ] If no local ref exists, the initial fetch is bounded by a default ~30s timeout and returns a clean, actionable error on timeout with diagnostics.
- [ ] Fetch/auth failures appear as warnings (`fetch_error`) but do not block IPC responses.
- [ ] Background refresh still updates cached state once auth/network is available.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/core.rs`, `src/daemon/git_worker.rs`, `src/git/sync.rs`
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Harden git sync state machine edge cases
description:

Sync/load/refresh pathways have overlapping but different logic. Edge cases (diverged refs, remote missing ref, local ahead, dirty during refresh, rewritten history) are not fully covered by tests. This risks subtle data loss or confusing behavior.

**Design**

Assert invariants: no local data loss, backups created, errors surfaced. Consider consolidating merge logic into a single path to reduce divergence.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests cover diverged/local-ahead/force-push scenarios.
- [ ] Failures are explicit; no silent state drops.
- [ ] Backups created when local history would be overwritten.
- [ ] Tests pass.

title: darin@dusk
Blocking freshness loads stall entire daemon
description:
**Problem**
`ensure_repo_fresh()` does a blocking `GitOp::Load` roundtrip on the state thread. Any slow fetch = every client request pauses. OK-ish for small repos, but a design footgun.

**Design**
Make refresh async:
- If repo is clean and refresh TTL hit, kick off background load
- Serve current cached state meanwhile
- Apply refreshed state when it arrives (maybe invalidate caches)
- Optionally: let queries request `fresh=true` when they really need it

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Slow git fetch doesnt block unrelated client requests
- [ ] Stale reads are bounded (TTL still respected eventually)
- [ ] Fresh flag available for queries that need it

darin@book
title: darin@book
CRDT join property tests (commutative/associative/idempotent)
description:
**Problem**
We don’t have property tests proving CRDT join algebra and invariants hold across random states. A subtle regression could cause silent data loss.

**Design**
Add proptest generators for CanonicalState (beads + tombstones + deps) and assert:
- join(a,b) == join(b,a)
- join(a, join(b,c)) == join(join(a,b), c)
- join(a,a) == a
- invariants hold after join (live ∩ tombstones = ∅, dep indexes consistent)
Use minimal valid field generation (safe ActorId/BeadId) and keep sizes small for speed.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Property tests added and deterministic
- [ ] No panics; invariants checked
- [ ] Tests run in CI

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/core/dep.rs, Cargo.toml
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Sync fails when migrating from beads-go in PR branch with existing store
description:
**Problem**
When migrating from beads-go to beads-rs in a PR branch, the sync doesn't work properly. Beads appear not to exist after migration.

**Root Cause**
The `migrate from-go` command bypasses the daemon entirely - it directly writes to `refs/heads/beads/store` using `SyncProcess` (src/cli/commands/migrate.rs:71-74). But the daemon caches state in memory per-remote in `RepoState`. After migration completes:
1. Git ref has the migrated state
2. Daemon still has empty/old cached state
3. CLI queries go through daemon, return stale data

**Design**
Add a `Request::Refresh` IPC command that tells the daemon to reload state from the git ref for a given repo. The migrate command should call this after successful migration.

Alternatively, migrate could go through the daemon IPC entirely, but that's more invasive.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] After `bd migrate from-go`, `bd list` shows the migrated beads
- [ ] Works even if daemon was already running before migration
- [ ] Test: migrate in a fresh clone, verify state is visible

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/commands/migrate.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
closed_reason: Fixed: added Request::Refresh and notify daemon after migration

Write migration guide for beads-go to beads-rs
description:
Document the migration path for users of the Go version to switch to Rust. Cover:

- Data format changes (if any)

This helps adoption and shows the project is mature enough to replace the Go version.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
WAL write failure must abort mutation + preserve state
description:
**Problem**
Mutations can succeed even when WAL writes fail. In `src/daemon/core.rs` `mark_dirty_and_schedule` logs a warning and proceeds, so a crash can lose acknowledged data. There is no rollback of in-memory state when WAL writes fail.

**Design**
Introduce a transactional mutation path that applies changes to a cloned `CanonicalState`, writes the WAL entry first, and only commits the new state + dirty flag on success. Add a dedicated OpError (e.g., `WalError`/`DurabilityError`) surfaced via IPC. Ensure WAL sequence only advances on successful write. Add a unit test that forces WAL write failure and verifies no state change + error return.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL write failures return an error and do not modify repo state
- [ ] WAL sequence/dirty flags remain unchanged on WAL failure
- [ ] Successful WAL writes still schedule sync
- [ ] Tests cover WAL failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/ops.rs, src/daemon/wal.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Persist root slug in meta.json; default new IDs to repo-name
description:
## What's Wrong
Bead IDs now support `<slug>-<suffix>` to match beads-go (repo-name slug), but new repos still default to `bd` because slug selection is inferred from existing state.

## Where
- src/daemon/executor.rs: preferred_bead_slug(...) falls back to "bd" when store is empty
- src/git/wire.rs: meta.json only stores format_version; no place to persist root slug

## Why It Matters
- Fresh repos should default to the repo-name slug (beads-go parity) or at least allow configuring it.
- Inferring slug from existing IDs fails for empty stores and is an implicit heuristic.

## Suggested Fix
- Add `root_slug` to `meta.json` (WireMeta + core Meta), and set it on init (derive from repo dir name or remote URL basename; sanitize).
- Use persisted `root_slug` for new ID generation.
- Decide compatibility strategy (format_version bump if needed; ensure old readers ignore unknown fields or gate by version).

## Acceptance
- After `bd init` in a repo named `foo`, new IDs default to `foo-...` (or a clearly documented rule).
- `bd create` in an empty store uses `meta.root_slug`, not a heuristic.

title: darin@dusk
Crash recovery integration test (kill -9 WAL replay)
description:
**Problem**
We still lack an integration test that proves WAL recovery works after an unclean daemon crash. Data loss here would be catastrophic.

**Design**
Add an integration test that:
1) creates a repo with a bare remote
2) starts the daemon (via bd create --json) and captures PID via IPC Ping
3) performs a mutation (bd create) and verifies WAL file exists
4) SIGKILL the daemon immediately (kill -9) before sync finishes
5) restart via any bd command (autostart)
6) assert the bead is present (bd list/show) and can be synced to remote
Use per-test XDG_RUNTIME_DIR + BD_WAL_DIR temp dirs to avoid cross-test leakage.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test passes reliably
- [ ] WAL replay is asserted (data present after restart)
- [ ] No flakiness in CI

**Files:** tests/critical_path.rs (or new tests/robustness.rs), src/daemon/ipc.rs (if needed)

description: darin@book
title: darin@dusk
Collapse live/tombstones into single BeadEntry map
description:
**Problem**
The invariant "live ∩ tombstones = ∅" is enforced by careful method design:
```rust
pub struct CanonicalState {
    live: BTreeMap<BeadId, Bead>,
    tombstones: BTreeMap<BeadId, Tombstone>,
    // ...
}
```

This relies on `insert`/`delete` being correct. A bug could violate the invariant.

**Design**
Make the invariant structurally impossible:
```rust
pub enum BeadEntry {
    Live(Bead),
    Tombstone(Tombstone),
}

pub struct CanonicalState {
    beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>,
    deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>,
}
```

Now a bead cannot be both live and tombstoned - the type system prevents it.

**Design Notes**
- Bigger refactor than the other type improvements
- Consider doing this when touching `state.rs` for other reasons
- `get_live`/`get_tombstone` become pattern matches on entry
- Iteration patterns change (`live.values()` → `beads.values().filter_map(...)`)
- Wire format (`src/git/wire.rs`) may need adjustment

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `BeadEntry` enum in `src/core/state.rs`
- [ ] `CanonicalState` uses single `beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>`
- [ ] All accessors (`get_live`, `get_tombstone`, iteration) updated
- [ ] Wire format handles the change
- [ ] Invariant comment updated to note its now structural
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/git/wire.rs

title: Durability gap: ops succeed before anything hits disk
description:
**Problem**
Mutations update in-memory state and schedule a sync later. If the process crashes or power dies before the debounce fires, acknowledged work is lost.

**Design Options**

*Option A:* Local WAL/journal
Append-only ops or periodic snapshot in the socket dir; replay on daemon start.

*Option B:* Immediate local-only git commit  
On each mutation, write a local-only beads commit immediately (durable), push/compact later. Adds commits but can squash in compaction pass.

**Design Notes**
User concern: Option B adds commits. Consider squash-on-push or a separate local ref that gets force-pushed after compaction.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Crash after acknowledged mutation doesnt lose data
- [ ] Recovery mechanism tested (kill -9 during burst, restart, verify state)
- [ ] Performance acceptable (write latency)

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
closed_reason: Implemented WAL-based durability. Replaced local git commits with atomic WAL file writes (~10x faster). Recovery on startup merges WAL with git state. Unit tests pass; full kill -9 integration test deferred.

darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Clock skew can break LWW ordering
description:
**Problem**
HLC relies on wall clock time. If a machine clock jumps backward or is far ahead, its stamps can dominate LWW merges and effectively overwrite other actors' updates. This can look like data loss.

**Design**
Persist the last-seen WriteStamp (per-repo) and clamp local wall_ms to >= last_seen.wall_ms. If local time moves backwards, advance logical counter instead of wall time. Add skew detection (warn if now << last_seen or now >> last_seen by threshold). Consider storing max observed stamp in meta or WAL to survive restarts.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Stamps remain monotonic across restarts and backward clock jumps.
- [ ] Skew detection is surfaced (status/log).
- [ ] Tests simulate backward/forward jumps and verify monotonicity + merge ordering.

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/core.rs

darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add derived indexes to avoid full dep scans
description:
Many queries do repeated full scans of deps (`deps_from`, `compute_blocked_by`, cycle detection, validation). Fine at 200 issues, painful at 20k.

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/daemon/query_executor.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
design:
**In-memory only** — indexes live in `RepoState`, never persisted to deps.jsonl.

**Rebuild on load:** When daemon starts or merges from git, iterate deps once to build:
```rust
struct DepIndexes {
    out_edges: BTreeMap<BeadId, Vec<(BeadId, DepKind)>>,  // from -> [(to, kind)]
    in_edges: BTreeMap<BeadId, Vec<(BeadId, DepKind)>>,   // to -> [(from, kind)]
}
```

**Incremental update:** In `apply_add_dep` / `apply_remove_dep`, push/remove from both maps.

**Functions to change:**
- `deps_from()` (state.rs:300) → lookup `out_edges[id]`
- `deps_to()` (state.rs:309) → lookup `in_edges[id]`  
- `compute_blocked_by()` (query_executor.rs:707) → iterate `in_edges` filtered by kind
- `would_create_cycle()` (executor.rs:1073) → BFS uses `out_edges` directly

Complexity drops from O(all deps) to O(neighbors per hop).

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
session_coordinator_boundary model hits counterexample for pending ingest property
description:
**Problem**
`session_coordinator_boundary` model reports a counterexample for the property "pending ingest starts at next expected" when running `cargo run --example session_coordinator_boundary -- check`. This indicates either the property is too strict for v0.5 semantics (buffered/out-of-order ingest) or the model logic is wrong.

**Design**
Re-run the model and capture the counterexample path. Decide whether the property should be relaxed (e.g., allow pending ingest to start earlier) or the model should be fixed to align with v0.5 gap buffering/prev deferral rules. Update the property or ingest logic accordingly and rerun the model to confirm no counterexample.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Reproduce the counterexample path from the model output.
- [ ] Decide whether the property or model logic is wrong and update accordingly.
- [ ] `cargo run --example session_coordinator_boundary -- check` completes with no counterexample for that property.

**Files:** `beads_stateright_models/examples/session_coordinator_boundary.rs`
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright state machine modeling for realtime Beads
description:
**Problem**
We need a tracked epic for the Stateright state machine model suite described in REALTIME_PLAN.md and beads_stateright_models/todo.md. Without it, modeling work is ad hoc and the sequence of invariants and model boundaries is not managed.

**Design Notes**
Use multiple small models at the seams: replication core, durability, idempotency, checkpoint lane, GC floor, identity and epoch guards, resource bounds, crash recovery. Each model should map counterexamples to integration tests.

**Files:** REALTIME_PLAN.md, beads_stateright_models/todo.md, beads_stateright_models/src, beads_stateright_models/examples
design:
**Design**
Build a model portfolio, ordered for maximum early signal, matching the todo list:
1) Realtime replication core: EVENTS, WANT, ACK, gaps, equivocation, monotonic seen map.
2) Durability semantics: applied vs durable watermarks, ReplicatedFsync(k) coordinator.
3) Idempotency and receipts: client_request_id mapping, PENDING vs COMMITTED, retry behavior, crash cut points.
4) Checkpoint lane: snapshot inclusion, included_heads, multi writer retry loop, truthful included watermarks.
5) GC markers and floors: ignore old events but advance contiguity and ACK.
6) Store identity and epoch mismatch guards across replication and checkpoint import.
7) Resource bounds and fairness: bounded buffers, round robin per namespace origin.
8) WAL crash recovery cut points: no origin_seq reuse, durable means survives crash.
9) Deterministic encoding and hashing: canonical CBOR and checkpoint hashing via property tests or tiny models.

Each model should live in beads_stateright_models/src or examples, keep state minimal, and reference matching sections in REALTIME_PLAN.md so counterexamples map to implementation changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Epic decomposed into per model beads with dependencies and priorities
- [ ] Each model in beads_stateright_models/todo.md is implemented or explicitly scoped to non Stateright tests with rationale
- [ ] Each model asserts the invariants listed in todo.md and references REALTIME_PLAN.md sections
- [ ] Every model counterexample that maps to a product bug is translated into a deterministic regression test in the main codebase
- [ ] Documentation exists in beads_stateright_models (README.md or similar) explaining how to run each model and interpret counterexamples

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: replication core (gaps/WANT/ACK/seen_map)
description:
**Problem**
The current Stateright examples split core replication invariants across multiple tiny models (gap_want, equivocation, watermarks). We need one integrated replication-core model that matches REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.4/§9.5/§9.6/§9.8 and §0.12 so counterexamples map directly to the actual ingest rules.

**Design Notes**
Use existing toy pieces as seeds: beads_stateright_models/examples/gap_want_machine.rs, beads_stateright_models/examples/equivocation_machine.rs, beads_stateright_models/examples/watermarks_machine.rs, and the shared types in beads_stateright_models/src/spec.rs or realtime_types_sketch.rs.
design:
**Design**
Create an integrated Stateright model for the replication core: multi-namespace (core, wf), multi-origin (>=2), unordered delivery, duplication allowed. Implement the Plan §9.4/§9.5 rules: contiguity-only advancement, bounded gap buffering, WANT emission, ACK monotonicity, and equivocation detection. The model should treat the event payload as a token + sha (Plan §0.6) and maintain a small state digest set to enforce idempotence.

Tie the model to plan language explicitly in comments: Plan §9.4 EVENTS ordering + contiguity, §9.5 ACK monotonicity, §9.6 WANT semantics, §9.8 gap bounds, §0.12 applied vs durable seen maps (even if durable==applied in this first model). Reuse OriginStreamState-like logic (realtime_types_sketch.rs) or port minimal versions into the model to avoid drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/repl_core_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover todo.md Model #1 invariants: no-gap ACK, idempotence, equivocation detection, monotonicity, origin ordering, and reachability
- [ ] Model explicitly references REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.4/§9.5/§9.6/§9.8 and §0.12 in doc comments
- [ ] Model runs via cargo run --example repl_core_machine (or equivalent) within reasonable timeout

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: admission + overload shedding priorities
description:
**Problem**
The plan defines admission priority and overload shedding (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19). There is no model to validate that local IPC mutations are prioritized over replication ingest and background work.

**Design Notes**
This is a lightweight model of a priority queue with bounded capacity.
design:
**Design**
Add a Stateright model that simulates an AdmissionController with prioritized queues: IPC mutations > replication ingest > WANT servicing > checkpoint/scrub (Plan §0.19). Model bounded queue capacity and drop/close behavior when overloaded.

Properties: local IPC is not starved by background work; replication is the first to shed under overload; no unbounded queues. Tie these to the plan invariants.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/admission_overload_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover priority ordering and overload shedding per REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example admission_overload_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: require_min_seen read gating + timeouts
description:
**Problem**
Read gating semantics (require_min_seen + wait_timeout_ms) are specified in REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 but not modeled. Without a model, it is easy to return stale reads or mis-handle retryable timeouts.

**Design Notes**
Model reads against applied watermarks and a bounded wait timer.
design:
**Design**
Create a Stateright model for read gating that uses applied watermarks as the truth (Plan §16.1). Include read requests with require_min_seen and wait_timeout_ms, and allow nondeterministic apply progress. Model should return: success if applied >= required, wait up to timeout, else return retryable error with current applied watermark.

Properties: no read is returned with applied < require_min_seen; timeout responses are retryable and include the current watermark snapshot.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/read_gating_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover correct gating and timeout semantics per REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example read_gating_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: durability semantics + ReplicatedFsync(k)
description:
**Problem**
We need a durability coordinator model that matches REALTIME_PLAN.md §10 and §0.12. Existing watermarks_machine.rs only models monotonic counters and does not exercise ReplicatedFsync(k), eligible replica selection, or timeout semantics.

**Design Notes**
This should align with Plan §10.1-§10.5 and the applied vs durable watermark distinction in §0.12.
design:
**Design**
Build a Stateright model that separates applied vs durable watermarks and explicitly models persistence as a boolean per event. Add a coordinator that waits for ReplicatedFsync(k), using an eligibility roster (Plan §10.2) and rejecting ineligible replicas. Include timeout behavior that returns retryable errors with receipts (Plan §10.5 + §0.11).

Leverage the existing watermarks_machine.rs for structure but extend to include: multiple replicas, a quorum counter, and ACK-driven durable advancement. Ensure ACKs cannot advance durable beyond locally persisted events (Plan §9.5 + §0.12).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/durability_quorum_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties enforce: no false durability, quorum correctness for k, no counting ineligible replicas, and honest timeout receipts (todo.md Model #2)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §10, §0.12, and §0.11
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example durability_quorum_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: idempotency + receipts with crash cut points
description:
**Problem**
Idempotency and receipts are correctness-critical (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11, §8.2, §6.5). There is no model today that covers client_request_id reuse, PENDING vs COMMITTED receipts, or crash-window retries.

**Design Notes**
Model the receipt state machine and crash cut points so retries are deterministic and do not mint new txn_id or stamps.
design:
**Design**
Create a Stateright model with a client_request_id key, request_sha256 guard, and PENDING/COMMITTED receipt states. Include crash cut points aligned to Plan §6.5 ordering (append, fsync, index commit, apply, receipt finalize, reply). Allow retries at any point and ensure the model returns the original receipt without minting new identifiers (Plan §0.11, §8.2).

Use small integers for stamps/txn_id but enforce the invariant that retries reuse the same values. Add explicit mismatch handling when the same client_request_id arrives with a different request digest (Plan §0.11).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/idempotency_receipt_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover: at-most-once per client_request_id, retry returns original receipt, no stamp remint, and request digest mismatch rejection (todo.md Model #3)
- [ ] Crash/restart scenarios are modeled for the cut points in REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.5
- [ ] Model doc comments reference §0.11, §8.2, and §6.5

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: checkpoint lane correctness + multi-writer
description:
**Problem**
We need a model for checkpoint correctness and convergence that reflects REALTIME_PLAN.md §13 and §0.12.1. There is no Stateright model covering included watermarks, included_heads, or multi-writer retry logic.

**Design Notes**
Keep the checkpoint data abstract (state_digest + included watermarks) but model the writer loop and import rules.
design:
**Design**
Build a Stateright model of the checkpoint lane: a register representing the checkpoint ref per group, multiple writers with non-FF failures, and import + merge logic. Represent each checkpoint as (state_digest, included watermarks, included_heads, store_epoch). Enforce Plan §13.9 import rules: durable watermarks advance to at least meta.included and included_heads seed head_sha (Plan §0.12.1).

Model the multi-writer retry loop (Plan §13.10): fetch, import+merge, re-export, retry until push succeeds. Reject epoch mismatch (Plan §2.1 + §13.9.1).

Keep JSONL/manifest/tar out of scope; this model focuses on semantic correctness (Plan §13.3-§13.9).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/checkpoint_lane_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover: truthful inclusion, import advances durable >= included, multi-writer convergence, and no cross-epoch merge (todo.md Model #4)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §13 and §0.12.1
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example checkpoint_lane_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: GC markers + floor semantics
description:
**Problem**
GC floor enforcement is correctness-sensitive but currently unmodeled. We need a Stateright model that matches REALTIME_PLAN.md §2.4 (NamespaceGcMarker) and the GC floor rules described in todo.md Model #5 so we do not accidentally stall contiguity or resurrect old state.

**Design Notes**
The model should separate state mutation from contiguity/ACK advancement.
design:
**Design**
Create a model that introduces NamespaceGcMarker events, tracks gc_floor_ms per namespace, and enforces the rule: events with event_time_ms <= gc_floor are ignored as state mutations but still advance contiguity and ACK (Plan §2.4 + §9.4). Use a tiny state digest (e.g., set of applied event_ids) to test resurrection behavior.

Allow nondeterministic emission of GC markers by the authority replica and ensure floor monotonicity (todo.md Model #5).

Reference the apply logic seam in realtime_types_sketch.rs or a small local helper to keep plan alignment.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/gc_floor_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover: floor monotonicity, old events do not mutate state, old events still advance contiguity/ACK, and deterministic convergence (todo.md Model #5)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §2.4 and §9.4
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example gc_floor_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: store identity + epoch guardrails across lanes
description:
**Problem**
Identity and epoch mismatch rules must be enforced consistently across replication, checkpoint import, and WAL header validation (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1, §2.1, §9.3, §9.10). The current identity_handshake model only covers the replication handshake path.

**Design Notes**
We need one model or a paired model set that proves the same guardrails apply in all lanes.
design:
**Design**
Extend or add a Stateright model that covers store_id and store_epoch gating across: (1) replication HELLO/WELCOME, (2) checkpoint import, and (3) WAL segment header validation. Model mismatches and assert hard-close or reject semantics in all lanes (Plan §9.10 error handling).

Reuse or build on beads_stateright_models/examples/identity_handshake.rs, but add explicit actions for checkpoint import and WAL header accept/reject. Ensure the same validation function is referenced in comments to map to the plan.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] identity_handshake model is extended or a new cross-lane model added (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/identity_epoch_machine.rs)
- [ ] Properties cover: no cross-store merge, no cross-epoch merge, and consistent rejection across replication/checkpoint/WAL paths (todo.md Model #6)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1, §2.1, §9.3, and §9.10

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: resource bounds + fairness across namespaces
description:
**Problem**
The plan mandates bounds (MAX_FRAME_BYTES, MAX_EVENT_BATCH_BYTES, gap buffer caps) and fairness across namespaces/origins (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19, §9.8). There is no model that checks starvation and bounded queues.

**Design Notes**
Model cost as small integers; focus on fairness and bounded buffers rather than exact byte sizes.
design:
**Design**
Build a Stateright model for bounded buffers and fair scheduling across namespaces/origins. The model should include: bounded gap buffer per origin, in-flight event limits, and a round-robin scheduler that selects per-(ns, origin) batches with caps (Plan §9.8).

Properties: buffers never exceed configured bounds (Plan §0.19), and no starvation for core when wf is high churn (todo.md Model #7).

The model can reuse limits constants from realtime_types_sketch.rs or define tiny constants in the example.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/fairness_bounds_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover bounded queues and no starvation for core under sustained wf churn (todo.md Model #7)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19 and §9.8
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example fairness_bounds_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: WAL crash recovery cut points
description:
**Problem**
Crash consistency and origin_seq uniqueness must hold across the append/fsync/index/apply/receipt pipeline (REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.5). There is no model that explores crash cut points and recovery behavior.

**Design Notes**
This is an abstract model of ordering, not a full WAL implementation.
design:
**Design**
Create a Stateright model that encodes the durability ordering from Plan §6.5: allocate seq, write record, fsync, index commit, apply, receipt finalize, reply. Allow a crash at each cut point and a recovery step that replays durable records and reconciles PENDING receipts (Plan §0.11).

Properties: no origin_seq reuse after recovery, LocalFsync receipts imply event survives crash, and recovery finds durable-but-unindexed events (todo.md Model #8).

Keep the state small (counters + flags) but align the cut points to the plan language.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New model file exists (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/wal_crash_recovery_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover no origin_seq reuse, receipt honesty, and recovery catch-up (todo.md Model #8)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.5 and §0.11
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example wal_crash_recovery_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Stateright: canonical hash/prev gating + unknown-key rules
description:
**Problem**
The hash/prev continuity and canonical CBOR rules (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.6, §0.6.1, §0.12.1, §9.4) are foundational. We have hash_prev_machine.rs, but it does not cover unknown-key preservation or the out-of-order buffering vs prev-check policy implied by §9.4.

**Design Notes**
This model should clarify how verification interacts with gaps and how canonicalness is enforced before WAL append.
design:
**Design**
Extend hash_prev_machine.rs or create a new model that adds: (a) a canonical/ non-canonical flag for incoming bytes, (b) unknown-key preservation as a requirement for hash verification, and (c) explicit policy for out-of-order events (buffered until prev is known vs immediate rejection). Tie that policy to Plan §9.4 and §0.12.1 in comments and pick the interpretation the plan intends.

Model invariants: wrong store => reject, sha mismatch => reject, non-canonical => reject, prev mismatch => reject, equivocation => reject. Ensure contiguity is only advanced after successful verification and (if buffered) after gaps are filled.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] hash_prev_machine.rs updated or a new model added (e.g., canonical_hash_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover: canonical-only acceptance, unknown-key preservation for hash validity, prev continuity per §0.12.1, and defined behavior for out-of-order events
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.6, §0.6.1, §0.12.1, and §9.4
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example canonical_hash_machine (or equivalent)

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Remove duplicate key from DepEdge
description:
**Problem**
`DepKey` is stored both as the map key AND inside `DepEdge.key`:
```rust
deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>

pub struct DepEdge {
    pub key: DepKey,      // <-- duplicates the map key
    pub created: Stamp,
    pub life: Lww<DepLife>,
}
```

This duplication means:
1. Wasted memory
2. Possible key/value mismatch bugs (though unlikely)

**Design**
Remove `key` from `DepEdge`:
```rust
pub struct DepEdge {
    pub created: Stamp,
    pub life: Lww<DepLife>,
}
```

Callers that need the key get it from the map iteration:
```rust
for (key, edge) in &state.deps {
    // key is available here
}
```

**Design Notes**
- Wire format needs to handle this - on serialization, include key; on deserialization, reconstruct
- `DepEdge::new` and `DepEdge::join` signatures change (no longer take/return key)
- Check all usages of `edge.key` and ensure key is available from context

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `DepEdge` no longer contains `key` field
- [ ] Wire format correctly serializes/deserializes (key comes from map, not struct)
- [ ] All `edge.key` usages updated to use key from iteration context
- [ ] `DepEdge::new`/`join` updated
- [ ] Tests pass

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Allow cycles for Related deps, keep DAG enforcement for Blocks
description:
**Problem**
`apply_add_dep` rejects ANY cycle regardless of dep kind. But "related" edges being acyclic is unnecessarily strict - related beads can absolutely reference each other cyclically.

**Design**
- `Related` and `DiscoveredFrom`: allow cycles (these are informational links)
- `Blocks` and `Parent`: enforce DAG (these have ordering semantics)

**Design Notes**
Open question: what about `DependsOn`? Could argue either way:
- DAG: "cant start A until B done" implies ordering
- Allow cycles: maybe you want mutual dependencies that resolve together?

Suggest: enforce DAG for `DependsOn` initially (safer), can relax later.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd dep add A B --kind=related` succeeds even if B->A exists
- [ ] `bd dep add A B --kind=blocks` still rejects cycles
- [ ] Tests for both cases

**Files:** src/daemon/executor.rs (apply_add_dep), src/core/dep.rs

Not a moral issue, just free perf + less churn.

title: darin@book
Add --parent flag to bd update command
description:
**Problem**
Cannot reparent a bead after creation. Beads created independently cannot be linked to an epic as children later.

Currently `--parent` only works at create time:
```
bd create "subtask" --parent=bd-epic
```

**Solution**
Add `--parent` flag to `bd update`:
```
bd update bd-xyz --parent=bd-epic
```

**Design Notes**
- Should validate parent exists and is not deleted
- Should prevent circular parent relationships
- Consider `--parent=""` or `--no-parent` to unparent a bead
- Parent changes should be LWW like other fields
- ID format: beads created with --parent get hierarchical IDs (bd-abc.1). Reparenting existing beads probably keeps their original ID rather than renumbering.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd update <id> --parent <epic-id>` works
- [ ] Invalid/deleted parent rejected with clear error
- [ ] Circular parent reference rejected
- [ ] Can unparent a bead (remove from epic)
- [ ] `bd show` reflects new parent
- [ ] `bd epic status` includes reparented beads
