title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Centralize dependency spec parsing
description:
**Problem**
Dependency spec parsing is duplicated in `src/cli/mod.rs` (`normalize_dep_specs`) and `src/daemon/executor.rs` (`parse_dep_specs`). The two implementations can drift (string format, DepKind parsing, error messages), which makes CLI validation diverge from daemon validation and complicates reasoning about dependency formats.

**Design**
Add a shared parser in core and use it from both CLI and daemon:
- Introduce a `DepSpec` type in `src/core/dep.rs` (or a small `dep_spec` module) with `{ kind: DepKind, id: BeadId }`.
- Implement `DepSpec::parse_list(raw: &[String]) -> Result<Vec<DepSpec>, CoreError>` that handles comma-separated lists and optional `kind:id` prefixes.
- Implement `DepSpec::to_spec_string()` for canonical formatting (`blocks:bd-123` or `bd-123` for default).
- Update `src/cli/mod.rs` to replace `normalize_dep_specs` with core parsing + `to_spec_string()` for normalized request strings.
- Update `src/daemon/executor.rs` to replace its local `parse_dep_specs` with core parsing and map errors to `OpError::ValidationFailed`.

**Design Notes**
Keep the current user-facing behavior (comma-separated specs, optional `kind:` prefix) and preserve existing error messages as much as possible. Use `DepKind::parse` and `BeadId::parse` in one place so the spec grammar is canonical.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI and daemon both rely on the core parser for dependency specs.
- [ ] Canonical formatting is unchanged for default `blocks` dependencies.
- [ ] Error messages remain user-friendly and point to the `deps` field.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Move label/parent updates into daemon ops
description:
**Problem**
CLI handlers perform read-modify-write for labels and parent relationships. `src/cli/commands/label.rs` fetches each issue then sends a full `Update` patch, and `src/cli/commands/update.rs` queries deps then removes/adds parent edges. This makes changes non-atomic, adds round trips, and splits core behavior across CLI and daemon.

**Design**
Add daemon-side ops for label and parent mutations and switch CLI to use them:
- Extend IPC `Request` with `AddLabels { repo, id, labels: Vec<String> }` and `RemoveLabels { repo, id, labels: Vec<String> }`, or a single `UpdateLabels { add, remove }` request.
- Add `SetParent { repo, id, parent: Option<String> }` that replaces existing parent edges in one mutation.
- Implement handlers in `src/daemon/executor.rs` that apply the changes atomically in a single WAL mutation, validating label formats and parent existence.
- Update CLI `label` commands to call the new label ops; update `update` to call `SetParent` when `--parent/--no-parent` is provided.
- Keep response payloads consistent (use `OpResult::Updated` or add a small label/parent result if needed for clarity).

**Design Notes**
This keeps CLI thin and centralizes behavior in the daemon. It also avoids subtle races (labels changed between fetch and update) and makes parent updates atomic.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI label add/remove no longer fetches issues; daemon applies label changes atomically.
- [ ] Parent updates are handled by a dedicated daemon op; CLI no longer manipulates deps directly.
- [ ] Label validation errors and missing parent errors remain user-friendly.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`, `src/cli/commands/label.rs`, `src/cli/commands/update.rs`, `src/api/mod.rs`

- Implement `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields(&self, fields: &mut BeadFields, stamp: &Stamp) -> Result<(), OpError>` in `src/daemon/ops.rs`.
- Move the current field-application logic (including label parsing and status -> workflow transition) into this helper.
- Update `Daemon::apply_update` to call the helper inside the WAL mutation.

**Design Notes**
Keep behavior identical: required fields cannot be cleared, label parsing stays strict, and status strings map to the same workflows with the same validation errors.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `Daemon::apply_update` delegates patch application to `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields`.
- [ ] Label validation and workflow transitions behave exactly as before.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.

title: Clarify prev_sha validation vs gap buffering in v0.5 replication
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN.md v0.5 says receivers must verify `prev_sha256` matches the current head before buffering/appending, but also mandates buffering out-of-order events and issuing WANT. For seq > durable+1, `prev_sha256` references seq-1 which is unknown until the gap fills, so the rule is ambiguous.

**Design**
Clarify the intended rule:
- Option A: allow buffering even if prev_sha is not yet verifiable; validate when the prefix arrives.
- Option B: require prev_sha to match the durable head (no buffering of future events).
Update plan text to remove ambiguity and align the replication/model implementations.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Spec text explicitly states when prev_sha must be validated for buffered events.
- [ ] Model/implementation updated to match clarified rule.

description: darin@book

Smart commit message threshold (5) is hardcoded

title: Git fetch should use explicit refspec + surface errors
description:
**Problem**
`remote.fetch(&["refs/heads/beads/store"], ...)` relies on libgit2 refspec defaults; it may only update FETCH_HEAD and leave `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store` stale. Fetch errors are also ignored, so the daemon can sync against stale remote state without any signal.

**Design**
Use an explicit refspec (`refs/heads/beads/store:refs/remotes/origin/beads/store`) and return/propagate fetch errors (or at least surface them in status/IPC). Keep a best-effort path for offline reads but make failures visible.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Fetch updates `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store`
- [ ] Fetch errors are surfaced (status/IPC/logs)
- [ ] Tests cover refspec + fetch failure behavior

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/git_worker.rs

init_beads_ref must not overwrite existing local ref
description:
**Problem**
`init_beads_ref` fetches and force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to remote even if a local ref already exists. If local has data, this can overwrite it.

**Design**
If local ref exists, treat init as a no-op (already initialized). Only fast-forward local when it does not exist. Never force-update without preserving local history. Add a test that ensures init does not modify an existing local ref.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] init is a no-op when local ref exists
- [ ] No forced updates on existing local refs
- [ ] Tests cover init with existing local ref
- [ ] Tests pass

darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Scheduler spawns unbounded threads on reschedule (threadpocalypse)
description:
**Problem**
`src/daemon/scheduler.rs` spawns a new thread on every schedule call, and reschedules do not cancel old sleepers. Under bursty ops, this devolves into "death by a thousand sleeping threads".

**Design**
Single timer mechanism:
- Keep a min-heap of `(deadline, remote)`
- State loop computes next deadline and uses `crossbeam::channel::after(next - now)` (or one dedicated timer thread)
- When rescheduling, update the heap entry (or push and ignore stale entries by checking current deadline in a map)

This makes the daemon "boring" which is good.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] At most 1 timer thread (or zero with channel-based approach)
- [ ] Rapid reschedules dont accumulate sleeping threads
- [ ] Stress test: 1000 rapid mutations, verify thread count stays bounded
- [ ] Existing sync behavior preserved (debounce still works)

**Files:** src/daemon/scheduler.rs
darin@book

**Problem**

Move the current field-application logic (including label parsing and status→workflow transition) into this helper. Update `Daemon::apply_update` to call the helper inside `apply_wal_mutation`.

**Design Notes**
Keep behavior identical: ignore `Patch::Clear` for required fields (title/description) and for labels (no-op), preserve current status validation and error messages.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `Daemon::apply_update` delegates patch application to `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields`.
- [ ] Label validation and workflow transitions behave exactly as before.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`

rustfmt.toml uses unstable options on stable
description:
## What's Wrong
cargo fmt prints warnings because rustfmt.toml sets unstable_features=true and group_imports=StdExternalCrate, which require nightly rustfmt.

## Where
- rustfmt.toml

## Why It Matters
Noisy output in CI/local workflows (for example, just release-patch runs cargo fmt -- --check). It can also cause formatting drift if contributors have different toolchains.

## Options
1. Remove unstable settings and rely on stable rustfmt defaults.
2. Pin nightly rustfmt in dev shell/CI to support the config.

Remote URL normalization should canonicalize ssh/https variants
description:
**Problem**
State sharing keys by normalized remote URL, but normalization is currently minimal (trim slash + parse). ssh/https variants (e.g., `git@github.com:org/repo` vs `https://github.com/org/repo`) can create split state.

**Design**
Canonicalize host + path across ssh/https/file URLs. Consider using `git2::Remote::url()` parsing or a dedicated normalizer with test cases.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] ssh/https URLs normalize to same key
- [ ] Test coverage for common URL variants

darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Persist WAL in durable data dir
description:
**Problem**
WAL files are stored under the daemon socket dir (often XDG_RUNTIME_DIR). Those locations are ephemeral and can be cleared on reboot/cleanup. If sync hasn't completed, a reboot can drop WAL and lose acknowledged mutations.

**Design**
Store WALs in a persistent data directory (XDG_DATA_HOME/beads-rs/wal). Keep socket in runtime dir but decouple WAL path. Add migration from legacy runtime WAL dir to new persistent dir on daemon startup, preserving newer WAL if both exist. Allow env override for tests.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL persists across daemon restarts/reboots (no runtime dir dependency).
- [ ] Legacy runtime WALs are migrated or preserved with a warning.
- [ ] Tests cover migration + legacy format.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal.rs, src/daemon/run.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add with_mutation helper for borrow-checker friendly mutations
description:
**Problem**
Borrow checker forces this awkward pattern:
1. Borrow `repo_state` to check something
2. Need `&mut self` to tick the clock
3. Drop borrow, re-borrow, and `unwrap()` because "I proved it exists earlier"

**Design**
Wrap mutation in a helper that ticks before handing out the borrow:
```rust
impl Daemon {
    fn with_mutation<R>(
        &mut self,
        loaded: &LoadedRemote,  // or repo: &Path + git_tx
        f: impl FnOnce(&mut RepoState, Stamp) -> Result<R, OpError>,
    ) -> Result<R, OpError> {
        let write = self.clock_mut().tick();
        let stamp = Stamp { at: write, by: self.actor().clone() };
        let rs = self.repo_state_mut(loaded);
        f(rs, stamp)
    }
}
```

Now `apply_update`/`apply_close`/`apply_claim` can do "check + mutate" in one borrow.

**Design Notes**
- Depends on `LoadedRemote` existing (bd-xxx) OR can take repo+git_tx and call ensure_repo_loaded internally
- Consider whether stamp should be passed in or returned for callers that need it after
- May want variants: `with_mutation` vs `with_mutation_loaded` depending on whether repo already loaded

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `with_mutation` helper exists in `src/daemon/mod.rs`
- [ ] At least one executor method uses it (start with simplest case like `apply_claim`)
- [ ] Pattern eliminates the "check, drop, tick, re-fetch, unwrap" sequence
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
closed_reason: Added with_mutation helper to Daemon, refactored apply_claim to use it. Pattern eliminates the 'check, drop, tick, re-fetch, unwrap' sequence.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Daemon restart on client version mismatch
description:
## What's Wrong
If an older `bd daemon run` process is already running, a newer `bd` CLI will connect to it and may hit confusing errors when behavior/protocol expectations change (e.g. bead ID parsing rules).

## Where
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: client connects to existing socket without verifying daemon version
- src/daemon/run.rs: daemon is spawned from current executable only when no daemon is reachable

## Why It Matters
Upgrading `bd` can silently keep using an older daemon until the socket disappears. This can break new features (like non-`bd-` id slugs) and makes failures look like user input errors.

## Suggested Fix
Add a lightweight handshake (e.g. Request::Ping returns daemon version/build hash). If mismatch with client, have client request shutdown + respawn, or refuse with a clear message.

---
Discovered while: implementing beads-go migration root slug support.

title: darin@dusk
CI docs drift: xtask/coverage references but no xtask crate
description:
## What's Wrong
Repo docs/config mention a coverage job and a `cargo xtask coverage` path, but the repo has no `xtask` crate and current workflows don't include the referenced coverage job.

## Where
- .github/AGENTS.md (mentions coverage + xtask)
- .cargo/config.toml (defines `xtask` alias)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (current actual CI)

## Why It Matters
Stale CI documentation misleads contributors and future agents. The `xtask` alias suggests functionality that doesn't exist.

## Suggested Fix
Either:
1) Add an `xtask/` crate and wire a real coverage job, or
2) Remove the alias and update .github/AGENTS.md to match reality.

## Acceptance
- CI docs accurately describe the workflows that actually run.
- No dead `cargo xtask` alias unless backed by a crate.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Fix collision resolution: winner can be deleted by resolution tombstone
description:
**Problem**
In `src/git/collision.rs`, collision resolution remaps the loser and writes a tombstone for the old ID using a "resolution stamp". But the merge rule in `core/state.rs` is: tombstone wins unless the beads `updated_stamp()` is strictly newer.

If the tombstone stamp is newer than the winner beads updated stamp (likely, since resolution stamp is generated "now"), **the winner gets deleted during join**. This violates spec §4.1.1 ("winner retains original id") and is catastrophic.

**Design Options**

*Option A (cleanest):* Lineage-scoped tombstones
Tombstone includes the losing beads `created` stamp (or content-hash) so it only kills that lineage, not the winner occupying the ID.

*Option B (minimal change):* Emit two stamps on collision resolution
- `t_del` for the loser-id tombstone  
- `t_win` for a no-op "bump" on the winner bead so `t_win > t_del`
This makes the winner survive the delete rule while preventing loser resurrection.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Deterministic test: collision where winner is older, loser is newer, resolution happens later
- [ ] Winner survives resolution in all timestamp orderings
- [ ] Loser cannot resurrect after resolution
- [ ] Spec compliance verified

**Files:** src/git/collision.rs, src/core/state.rs
